content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of memory-augmented active learning agents can be reframed as a nested agent architecture, where the long-term memory store itself acts as an autonomous meta-agent that actively selects which new experience data to encode or prune, rather than being a passive storage layer controlled by the main task agent. This meta-agent within memory uses curiosity-driven reward signals aligned to the main agent’s long-term learning objectives to prioritize active sample selection, turning static memory into a self-improving active learning system.
